summary.what: Load provenance config + doctor surfaces unset user.email
summary.why: Phase 4 connects the abstract classifier from phase 3 to real environment state. The big risk in this change is the existing-test inheritance problem — NewStorage reading the host's user.email and falsely classifying mock commits as foreign-author. Hit it immediately when I ran 'just check' and three test suites failed. Fixing it by resetting newTestStorage's provenance to zero is the right pattern because (a) it preserves all existing test behavior, (b) tests that want provenance opt in explicitly, matching the same pattern v0.22.7 used for skipMessages, (c) it surfaces the load-bearing semantic: provenance must be explicit per Storage, not implicit.
summary.how: Three new pieces: git.ConfigUserEmail() helper (returns empty on any error — the safe-degradation contract), ledger.LoadProvenanceConfig(now) that builds the config from real environment state with a 24h DefaultSessionWindow constant, and a doctor check in cmd/timbers/doctor_session.go that warns when user.email is unset. NewStorage now calls LoadProvenanceConfig at construction. newTestStorage zeros out s.provenance after NewStorage so the inherited-config problem doesn't bite. Two doctor tests use real temp repos with GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null to defeat host config leakage (which was leaking robert.bergman@gmail.com from my user-level git config during initial runs).

notes: Considered an env-var override for ConfigUserEmail to make the unset case more controllable in tests — rejected because the GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null pattern works for the empty case and the set case can configure user.email locally. Considered making LoadProvenanceConfig take a *Storage or wiring it as a method on Storage — rejected because a free function is simpler and the constructor is the only caller in production. The 'phase 6 will extend this to honor session-window directive' is the explicit handoff to the next phase — directive parsing is out of scope here.
